routes.auth module¶

Authentication Routes Module¶

This module defines the API endpoints for user authentication, including signup, login, email confirmation, and password reset flows.

routes.auth.confirm_email(token: str, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Confirms a user’s email address using a verification token.

Parameters:

  • token (str) – The verification JWT token sent to the user’s email.

  • db (Session) – The database session.

Raises:

HTTPException – 400 if the token is invalid or user not found.

Returns:

Success message.

Return type:

dict

async routes.auth.forgot_password(body: RequestResetEmail, background_tasks: BackgroundTasks, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Initiates the password reset process by sending a reset link via email.

This operation is performed as a background task to ensure quick API response.

Parameters:

  • body (RequestResetEmail) – The user’s email address to request a reset for.

  • background_tasks (BackgroundTasks) – FastAPI background tasks manager.

  • db (Session) – The database session.

Returns:

A generic message indicating the email has been sent if the user exists.

Return type:

dict

routes.auth.login(body: OAuth2PasswordRequestForm = Depends(dependency=None, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Authenticates a user and returns access and refresh tokens.

Checks email existence, confirmation status, and password validity.

Parameters:

  • body (OAuth2PasswordRequestForm) – OAuth2 compatible form containing username (email) and password.

  • db (Session) – The database session.

Raises:

HTTPException – 401 if credentials are invalid or email is not confirmed.

Returns:

A dictionary containing access_token, refresh_token, and token_type.

Return type:

dict

routes.auth.me(request: Request, user: User = Depends(dependency=<bound method Auth.get_current_user of <services.auth.Auth object>>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Returns the profile information of the currently authenticated user.

This endpoint is rate-limited to 5 requests per minute.

Parameters:

  • request (Request) – The incoming HTTP request (required for rate limiting).

  • user (User) – The current authenticated user obtained from the JWT token.

Returns:

The user profile object.

Return type:

User

routes.auth.reset_password(body: ResetPassword, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Resets the user’s password using a valid reset token.

Parameters:

  • body (ResetPassword) – Contains the reset token and the new password.

  • db (Session) – The database session.

Raises:

HTTPException – 404 if the user associated with the token is not found.

Returns:

Success message.

Return type:

dict

async routes.auth.signup(body: UserSchema, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Registers a new user in the system and sends a confirmation email.

Parameters:

  • body (UserSchema) – User registration data (username, email, password).

  • db (Session) – The database session.

Raises:

HTTPException – 409 if the account already exists.

Returns:

The created user information.

Return type:

User
